title: Tesla driver faces manslaughter charges over Texas crash that killed a woman inside her home
summary: The man whose Tesla struck and killed a woman inside her Texas home last month is now facing manslaughter charges, as reported earlier by The Wall Street Journal and local news outlet KHOU 11. 44-year
content:
The man whose Tesla struck and killed a woman inside her Texas home last month is now facing manslaughter charges, as reported earlier by The Wall Street Journal and local news outlet KHOU 11. 44-year-old Michael Butler was arrested on Wednesday and claimed to have been driving his Model 3 using Tesla's Full-Self Driving (FSD) system at the time of the crash, according to an arrest affidavit.
The court document includes the officer saying that data extraction from Butler's phone found several FSD-related Google searches from May 2026: "Tesla fsd not aggressive enough 2026 model," "tesla fsdnot [sic] aggressive enough 2026," "tesla fsdnot [s …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Meta has a new app called Pocket that is absolutely nothing like the old Pocket
summary: Mozilla shut down the well-loved read-it-later Pocket app last year, and now Meta is launching an app called Pocket with an entirely different, AI-focused pitch: this new app lets you make and share l
content:
Mozilla shut down the well-loved read-it-later Pocket app last year, and now Meta is launching an app called Pocket with an entirely different, AI-focused pitch: this new app lets you make and share little interactive "gizmos" built from an AI prompt, as reported by Business Insider. 
Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is all in on AI as the new social media, and he's previously described a vision of how users could use AI to make interactive experiences and share them with people. The launch of Pocket appears to be one manifestation of that idea, and it follows Meta hiring engineers from a company called Atma Sciences Inc., which made an app called  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: The best July 4th sales we found so far
summary: July 4th sales are typically a precursor to what we’d see during a mid-July Prime Day, but obviously things are flipped around this year. Last week’s big Prime Day sale is over, yet there are a number
content:
July 4th sales are typically a precursor to what we’d see during a mid-July Prime Day, but obviously things are flipped around this year. Last week’s big Prime Day sale is over, yet there are a number of familiar deals still poking around in the week leading up to the nation’s birthday. Best Buy is hosting its own 4th of July sale, for example. And, if you’re on the hunt for tech and gear for the outdoors, REI is hosting a sale on products that will last through the week. There are even some Apple deals you can get, some of which are selling at pre-price hike levels.
We’ll be logging the best July 4th sales we find this week below, keeping the list updated with notable discounts as we spot them.
Asus ROG Falcata
The Falcata is stuffed with features (wired / wireless connectivity, split ergo design, Hall effect customization), and it’s a bargain to get it all at Best Buy’s 4th of July pricing. Read our review.

Where to Buy:

 $349.99 $214.99 at Best Buy

11-inch iPad Air M3
The last-gen 11-inch iPad Air comes with Apple’s M3 chip and GPU upgrades. It’s available in dark gray, blue, purple, and a “starlight” cream shade. Read our review.

Where to Buy:

 $749 $499 at Best Buy (128GB, Wi-Fi + LTE)

LG C5 OLED TV
LG’s C5 features a 120Hz panel, along with four HDMI 2.1 ports and support for both AMD FreeSync Premium and Nvidia G-Sync. The gamer-friendly TV also packs all the hallmarks of a modern OLED, including deep black levels and sublime contrast.

Where to Buy:

 $2699.99 $1299.99 at Amazon (65-inch)
 $1999.99 $1299.99 at Best Buy (65-inch)

Apple AirTags (second-generation)
Apple’s latest AirTag features an upgraded ultra-wideband chip for more precise location tracking, a noticeably louder speaker, and an expanded Bluetooth range. Read our review.

Where to Buy:

 $99 $89 at Amazon (pack of four)
 $99 $89 at Best Buy (pack of four)
 $128.99 $99.99 at Costco (pack of five)

OhSnap MCON controller
The MCON is one of the best mobile controllers for iPhones. While t

title: Weber marks down grills and griddles to their best prices ever for July 4th
summary: Weber is offering significant July 4th discounts on grills and griddles, including the Spirit E-325 propane model at $499 (down from $549) and the Slate 30-inch griddle at $649.
content:
The Weber Spirit E-325 grill with three burners. | Image: Weber	

If our recent Decoder interview with Weber Blackstone CEO Roger Dahle has you craving freshly grilled meats or veggies, Weber just so happens to have a variety of grills, smokers, griddles, and accessories selling at big discounts ahead of the July 4th holiday. It makes a bunch of models, but we’ve landed on a range of grills and griddles at varying price points. Weber is cheaper than other retailers by about $50 on most of these picks, but we’ve noted where you may be able to find them at a comparable price from local hardware stores like Home Depot or Ace.
For occasional cookouts in the backyard, the Spirit E-325 is a basic but reliable option with three burners. It’s available in either propane for $499 (usually $549) or natural gas for $549 (usually $599), a signifcant markdown considering how rarely they go on sale. The grilling surface is 360 square inches, enough space for a few burgers for you and your friends, and there’s a 90 square inch warming rack for toasting buns or for more delicate items that you don’t want to be as charred. This version features two searing zones with higher heat output for finishing steaks, a nice addition over the more basic $449 E-310.
Weber Spirit E-325

Where to Buy:

 $549 $499 at Weber (propane)
 $599 $549 at Weber (natural gas)

If you’re looking for a flattop with a drip catch for oil, there’s a $50 discount available on the Slate 30-inch rust-resistant griddle, bringing the price down to $649, which beats Home Depot’s price by $50. The cooking surface can reach up to 500 degrees and heats up in minutes, with wind guards to make sure everything cooks quickly and evenly. The 30-inch griddle is a good pick for a big party as it can cook about 22 burgers at once, while the larger 36-inch version can cook 30. Only the propane version of the 30-inch griddle is discounted, so you’ll have to go with either a larger or smaller version if you have a natural gas conne

title: The video game disc is dead
summary: Sony's decision to end PlayStation disc production marks the end of physical game media dominance, reflecting the industry-wide shift toward digital distribution across major console manufacturers.
content:
For decades, to be a gamer was to accumulate a lot of stuff. Consoles, controllers, accessories, weird VR gloves that never worked properly, but mostly the games themselves. Over the years, games have come in every shape and size you can imagine. And now that era appears to be ending.
On this episode of The Vergecast, David and Nilay talk about Sony's plan to end production of PlayStation discs, Microsoft's ongoing strategy for digitizing games, and in general the end of physical game media. It all makes a certain kind of business sense - and these companies are all desperately searching for anything that makes business sense - but it might …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Influencer screenings aren’t going away
summary: Movie studios including Universal continue hosting advance screenings for influencers and content creators, recognizing their significant role in modern entertainment media coverage.
content:
For a few days, it seemed like Universal decided that there would be no advanced screenings of Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey for influencers. But on Monday, influencers sat alongside traditional critics and journalists at special showings of The Odyssey specifically for the associated press junket. Despite what it may have looked like, Universal was not going back on its word. The studio was simply embracing an undeniable reality of the modern entertainment press.
Much as it may pain some to admit, influencers and content creators have become a significant part of our media ecosystem - one that offers film studios an invaluable way to rea …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Godox’s feature-packed key light is down to its best price yet
summary: If you don’t want to spend $180 on the Elgato Key Light, the Godox ES45 Desktop LED Key Light is a more affordable option that offers nearly as much brightness and plenty of features, including adjust
content:
If you don’t want to spend $180 on the Elgato Key Light, the Godox ES45 Desktop LED Key Light is a more affordable option that offers nearly as much brightness and plenty of features, including adjustable brightness and color temperature. Right now, it’s cheaper than usual for the first time this year, selling for $119 ($20 off) at Amazon and B&H Photo.
Godox ES45 key light

Where to Buy:

 $139 $119 at Amazon
 $139 $119 at B&H Photo
 $139 $119 at Dodd Camera

The ES45 is a worthwhile purchase for streamers, or really anyone who wants to look their best on camera. You can position the light to shine directly on you, or aim it at your wall for some even bias lighting that creates a glow effect in your room that’s easier on the eyes. It can go between a warm 2,800K to a cooler, daylight-like color temperature. 
One feature it has over Elgato’s popular Key Light is an included wireless 2.4GHz remote, which lets you easily adjust the lighting without having to fiddle with desktop or mobile apps. The remote automatically recharges when you reconnect it magnetically to the light. The ES45 also comes with a desk clamp that requires no tools to install, letting you attach it to a desk that’s up to two inches thick.
Three more Verge-approved deals
Speaking of brightening up your space, Govee’s Outdoor Smart Wall Light is on sale for $54.99 ($45 off) at Best Buy. Designed for outdoor spaces like patios, the IP65-rated lights offers up to 1,500 lumens of warm or cool white light, and can display three colors at once. The lights support Matter, too, making them easy to control with Amazon Alexa, Google Assistant, or other compatible smart home platforms. Plus, you get access to over 45 preset scene modes, though you can also create your own custom lighting effects in the Govee app.
Just in time for your exciting summer adventures, the capable Insta360 X5 has dropped to a new low price of $434.99 ($115 off) at Amazon, B&H Photo, and from the Insta360 store. The 360-degree action

zh.title: Godox功能丰富的按键灯降至迄今最低价格
zh.summary: 如果你不想花180美元购买Elgato按键灯，Godox ES45桌面LED按键灯是一个更经济实惠的选择，它提供了几乎相同的亮度和许多功能，包括调整选项。

title: AI won’t save advertising, says Digitas’ Amy Lanzi
summary: Amy Lanzi, CEO of Digitas North America, argues that AI won't be a solution to the advertising industry's challenges, pushing back against widespread hype at Cannes Lions. She emphasizes that her role is driving business results through data and analytics rather than buying into AI-generated advertising content.
content:
We’ve got a special Decoder today — I had the chance to talk with Amy Lanzi, the CEO of Digitas North America, in front of a live audience at the Uber Villa at the Cannes Lions advertising festival in the South of France. I know, it’s a hard gig, but I do it for you.
Amy has been on Decoder three times now, and she’s one of my favorite people to chat with — she is clear-eyed about what the advertising industry really is and does for brands and what all the money sloshing around the ad-supported internet really accomplishes. You’ll hear her say that she thinks the traditional chief marketing officer role is done for and that her job is driving business results using data and analytics. 



Verge subscribers, don’t forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Decoder wherever you get your podcasts. Head here. Not a subscriber? You can sign up here. 
That might sound straightforward, but it was a shocking statement at Cannes, which is where the entire advertising industry gathers every year, drinks rosé, and convinces itself of outrageous nonsense. This year, the big trends were creators, and, of course, AI. And Amy, alongside her parent company Publicis, aren’t holding back when it comes to calling out the AI nonsense for what it is.
Publicis actually put out an ad before Cannes listing all the false promises being made about AI when it comes to advertising, so I asked Amy about that, and what AI might actually be good for, beyond just generating slop and slop headlines. After all, Meta and the rest of the big platforms were all at Cannes talking about generating more and more ads with AI — something that threatens almost every other company in the industry. 
Of course, we also talked about the creator economy and how all the creators at Cannes were openly calling themselves marketers — essentially turning themselves into small ad agencies of their own. On top of that, the biggest creators in the world almost all launch their own products — something Amy and Digitas see

zh.title: 人工智能无法拯救广告行业，Digitas首席执行官Amy Lanzi表示
zh.summary: Digitas北美首席执行官Amy Lanzi辩称，人工智能不会成为解决广告行业挑战的方案，这与戛纳电影节上广泛传播的炒作相反。她强调，她的角色是通过数据和分析推动业务成果，而不是相信AI生成的广告内容。

title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
summary: Microsoft has launched its own AI deployment company with a $2.5 billion commitment, following similar investments from Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic. The move reflects growing demand for infrastructure to support AI model deployment at scale.
content: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

zh.title: 微软推出自有AI部署公司，承诺投入25亿美元
zh.summary: 微软推出了自有的AI部署公司，承诺投入25亿美元，跟随亚马逊、OpenAI和Anthropic的类似投资。此举反映了支持大规模AI模型部署基础设施的日益增长的需求。

title: Tesla saw a massive sales jump in the second quarter
summary: Tesla delivered more than 480,000 electric vehicles in the second quarter, boosted by geographic expansion and lower-priced versions of the Model 3, Model Y, and Cybertruck. The sales surge signals strong demand for Tesla's lineup despite ongoing competition in the EV market.
content: The company delivered more than 480,000 EVs globally, seemingly thanks to geographic expansion and cheaper versions of the Model 3, Model Y, and Cybertruck.

zh.title: 特斯拉第二季度销售大幅增长
zh.summary: 特斯拉在第二季度交付了超过48万辆电动汽车，受益于地理扩张和Model 3、Model Y和Cybertruck的低价版本。销售激增表明尽管电动车市场竞争激烈，但特斯拉产品线仍有强劲需求。

title: Rivian raises EV sales forecast as Q2 production ramps up
summary: Rivian raised its 2026 electric vehicle sales forecast following increased production in the second quarter and the recent launch of its R2 SUV. The company now expects to ship thousands more vehicles than previously projected by year-end.
content: The company now expects to ship a few thousand more vehicles by the end of 2026 than it previously expected, after launching its R2 SUV last month.

zh.title: Rivian在第二季度产量提升之际提高电动汽车销售预测
zh.summary: 在第二季度产量增加以及最近推出R2 SUV的推动下，Rivian提高了2026年电动汽车销售预测。该公司现在预计到年底的交付量将比之前预计的增加数千台。

title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
summary: A programmer created an automated script using OpenClaw and Claude code to reach potential dating matches on Instagram, demonstrating both the capability and questionable ethics of using AI for relationship-seeking at scale.
content: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.

zh.title: 没错，我们现在用OpenClaw来约会了
zh.summary: 一位程序员使用OpenClaw和Claude代码创建了自动化脚本，在Instagram上接触潜在的约会对象，既展示了使用人工智能大规模寻求恋爱关系的能力，也引发了伦理问题。

title: Mystery box shows are complicated for everyone — even the actors
summary: The complexity of Apple TV's Silo is so intricate that even showrunner Graham Yost has made continuity errors during production, requiring actors and translation teams to catch mistakes. The sci-fi thriller's layered narrative creates challenges for everyone involved in bringing it to screen.
content:
Silo is such a complicated show that even its showrunner gets confused sometimes. While filming the final seasons of the Apple TV sci-fi thriller, Graham Yost remembers two instances where he messed up details: once it was an actor who realized that a conversation they were about to shoot should've already taken place, the other involved the Japanese localization team pointing out that a subtitle didn't match what was going on onscreen. In both instances, the problem was ultimately fixed, but Yost's reaction was the same: "Oh shit, you're right."
Keeping everything straight is one of the big challenges of working on such a complex series, a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 悬念盒剧对所有人都很复杂——甚至对演员也是如此
zh.summary: Apple TV的《Silo》情节极其错综复杂，连制片人Graham Yost在制作过程中都犯过时间线错误，需要演员和翻译团队来发现问题。这部科幻惊悚剧的多层叙事为参与制作的每个人都带来了挑战。

title: Google’s AI buildout drove 37% increase in electricity use in 2025
summary: Google's expansion of AI data centers drove a 37 percent increase in electricity consumption during 2025, raising questions about the environmental cost of the company's AI buildout. The company is attempting to offset emissions through clean energy investments and efficiency measures.
content: Google tries balancing AI data center emissions with clean energy efforts.

zh.title: 谷歌AI建设导致2025年电力使用增长37%
zh.summary: 谷歌AI数据中心的扩展在2025年导致电力消耗增加37%，引发了关于该公司AI建设环境成本的疑问。该公司正在尝试通过清洁能源投资和效率措施来抵消排放。

title: BitTorrent’s disastrous, legendary, and controversial story
summary: BitTorrent, the revolutionary peer-to-peer file-sharing protocol that turned 25 years old, fundamentally changed the internet by enabling massive piracy and upending the entertainment industry. The technology's dual-use nature as both a legitimate tool and piracy enabler defined its controversial legacy.
content:
Twenty-five years ago today, a young, little-known programmer by the name of Bram Cohen fired off a short message to a mailing list for peer-to-peer enthusiasts. "My new app, BitTorrent, is now in working order, check it out here," Cohen wrote, followed by a link to his personal website.
"What's BitTorrent, Bram?" the founder of the list asked in response.
Cohen never bothered to reply. The world would find out soon enough.
In the following years, BitTorrent quickly became the world's most popular file-sharing app, unleashing a massive wave of piracy that upended Hollywood forever. At one point, BitTorrent was said to be responsible for a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: BitTorrent的灾难性、传奇性和争议性故事
zh.summary: 已有25年历史的革命性点对点文件共享协议BitTorrent通过促成大规模盗版和颠覆娱乐产业，从根本上改变了互联网。该技术作为合法工具和盗版启用器的双重用途性质定义了其有争议的遗产。

title: OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has proposed giving the Trump administration a 5 percent ownership stake in the company as a strategy to ease political tensions and share AI's financial upside with the public. Based on OpenAI's latest $852 billion valuation, the stake would be worth approximately $42.6 billion.
content:
OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, according to the Financial Times.
CEO Sam Altman argued that giving the public a financial interest in the company would be the best way to share the upside of AI, the FT reported, citing two unnamed people familiar with the talks. He's said to have first pitched the idea to Trump early last year.
Altman reportedly suggested the 5 percent figure. Based on OpenAI's latest funding round, which ended with the company valued at $852 billion, that stake would be worth …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: OpenAI提议向特朗普政府提供AI繁荣的5%股份
zh.summary: OpenAI首席执行官Sam Altman提议向特朗普政府提供该公司5%的所有权股份，以此作为缓解政治紧张关系并与公众分享AI财务收益的策略。根据OpenAI最新的8520亿美元估值，该股份的价值约为426亿美元。

title: Editorial: It's time to step up and have your say for science
summary: An Ars Technica editorial urges readers to submit comments on a proposed rule that would place political appointees in charge of scientific decisions. Public engagement in the regulatory process is framed as essential to protecting science from political interference.
content: Your comments on a dangerous rule putting politicals in charge of science can matter.

zh.title: 社论：是时候站出来为科学发声了
zh.summary: 一篇《Ars Technica》社论敦促读者就一项拟议规则提交意见，该规则将把科学决策权交给政治任命者。公众参与监管过程被认为是保护科学免受政治干扰的关键。

title: Amazon has enough satellites to launch its Starlink competitor
summary: Amazon Leo has deployed 396 satellites in low-Earth orbit, reaching the threshold needed to offer continuous internet service as a competitor to SpaceX's Starlink. The company remains on track to launch commercial service by mid-2026, though initial performance may not match SpaceX's established network.
content:
Payloads of Amazon’s Leo satellites sorted by launch vehicle. | Image: Amazon	

Amazon says it now has enough satellites operating in low-Earth orbit to light up its Starlink internet competitor. With last night's launch, Amazon Leo has 396  satellites deployed, which is "enough to support continuous service across initial latitudes," according to Chris Weber, VP heading up business and product for Amazon Leo. That puts the company on track to meet its "mid-2026" target for commercial availability. Just don't expect miracles on day one.
SpaceX went live with its "Better than nothing beta" back in 2020 when it had almost 900 satellites operating in low-Earth orbit. It initially served a narrow band of users in the upper …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 亚马逊拥有足够的卫星推出其Starlink竞争产品
zh.summary: 亚马逊Leo已在近地轨道部署396颗卫星，达到提供持续互联网服务以与SpaceX Starlink竞争所需的门槛。该公司计划在2026年中期推出商业服务，尽管初期性能可能不会达到SpaceX已建立网络的水平。

title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
summary: Indian entrepreneur Bhavin Turakhia is investing $30M of his own capital in Neo, his fifth venture, to build an AI-powered alternative to Microsoft Office and Google Apps. The startup aims to disrupt the productivity software market by combining enterprise software with artificial intelligence capabilities.
content: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office, Google Apps with AI.

zh.title: 印度科技大亨投入3000万美元自有资金打造微软Office的AI替代品
zh.summary: 印度企业家巴温·特拉基亚正投入3000万美元自有资金到他的第五个创业项目Neo中,以构建Microsoft Office和Google Apps的AI驱动替代品。这家初创公司致力于通过结合企业软件和人工智能能力来颠覆生产力软件市场。
